figures.caption: Table 1: Comparison of rates for convex decentralized stochastic optimization. Here N is the total number of stochastic gradient samples, M is the number of distributed nodes, ρ is the spectral gap, σ denotes the local noise scale, ζ⋆ denotes data heterogeneity measured at the optimum, ζ denotes a global heterogeneity bound, and c is a negative-eigenvalue parameter appearing in the gradient-tracking analysis of Koloskova et al. [12]. The table suppresses numerical constants, smoothness/radius parameters, and most logarithmic factors.
